body:
Continued per "proceed, do all missing updates" + "system unity pass": SLS(75) -> SLS(78)/9.92.

WARRANT#71 (keys-3 item 16): HH/HL/LH/LL structure-sequencing grammar over swing pivots, ATR-scaled tolerance, sequence-break count. Reused existing swing-detection primitives without touching the shared structure global other systems depend on.

WARRANT#72 (system unity pass): found LE_ComputePosteriorEdge/CappedKellySize (orphaned since WARRANT#44) and this session's own Keys-3 functions all compiled clean but were never exercised anywhere, even diagnostically. Added Forge_ReportSystemUnity() wiring them all into the existing ghost-validation report -- including a real cross-lane composition never run before: forge-indicate's relationship-coherence measure now feeds forge-edge-2's live-cell decision function directly.

WARRANT#73/74: Keys-3 scale-selection diagnostic (item 18) + forge-indicate Sigma-convergence status report (r7 continuation).

All compiled 0 errors, diff-reviewed (one was a same-session signature revision to code shipped earlier this same pass, not a regression against anything previously relied on -- flagged honestly in the ledger).

ONE THING DELIBERATELY NOT DONE: real PBO. I read the actual Phase 3B search loop before deciding -- it's the 50,000-draws-per-regime global weight search, which has a DOCUMENTED real prior incident (Windows killed the terminal mid-run at draw ~30000 on 2026-07-14, already patched once for that). Adding per-candidate fold tracking there means touching the single most fragile hot loop in the file. That's categorically different risk from everything else shipped this session, so I held it rather than touch it by default -- want me to go ahead anyway, or leave it for a dedicated, careful pass?

Attaching final SLS(78) mq4+ex4 and the updated forge_ledger.json (58 passes).
